text: During a single incision laparoscopic appendectomy, the surgeon introduces the sieve port using an open technique. The appendix is held by a grasper, and the bowel loop appears slightly distended, creating limited space. The surgeon uses a harmonic scalpel to carefully separate the mesoappendix, ensuring the tip does not contact the nearby bowel.

The surgeon uses a harmonic device to carefully dissect the appendicular artery, minimizing collateral damage. Traction is applied to expose the base of the appendix, which is then separated up to its base during the single incision laparoscopic appendectomy.

The surgeon applies Mishra's extracorporeal knot to the base of the appendix using an articulating grasper for traction. The knot is tightened with a knot pusher, ensuring no remnant of the stump is left. The remaining stump is sealed with a harmonic device to prevent spillage, and the knot is cut with scissors.

The appendix is carefully separated from its base, ensuring the mucosa is cauterized to prevent any mucosal remnants. The surgeon uses a grasper to hold the appendix, and the entire sealed structure is removed from the surgical site.
